title: Reset progress cleanly on playlist navigation
summary: 0.6.37 keeps Next and Previous boring again by resetting visible timeline state as soon as playback moves to a different movie, instead of briefly carrying the prior clip's progress through prepare.
changes: Raw standalone player loads now zero the video timeline immediately when a new media path is loaded.
Hosted standalone player loads now reset seek, timeline, and A-B state consistently before preparing a new media path.
Visible player readback now reports zero timeline while a new movie is still preparing, instead of showing stale progress from the previous clip.
reasoning: The remaining A-B navigation concern looked like progress was being preserved across a real media change even though A-B itself was clearing.
The likely culprit was stale timeline visibility during prepare, not a new transport policy change, so the fix stays in the media-change reset seam.
Raw and hosted paths should not diverge here, even though the current live test seam is raw core-player.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: When Next or Previous actually changes movies, progress should jump back to the beginning immediately instead of looking like it carried over.
The new movie should start from the beginning with A-B cleared, rather than inheriting stale visible timeline state during load.
knownIssues: This slice does not change demo3 control placement, slider/state readback, or text display formatting.
This slice does not add the later A-B incremental scrub behavior.
